parsed_review.summary: CP00-693 read-only review: PASS. Descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I P09 synthetic-fixture-tail / test-golden / Hermes-evidence / Claude-review / closeout-handoff bridge (84 units, risk C) honestly claims production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, pending this hardened read-only review (manifest/construction-inspection/command-evidence/adjudication all consistent at pending_review). Distributions match the frozen CP693 REQUIREMENTS, manifest, plan_binding_snapshot, coverage validator, and model tests: 84 = implementation 55 + claude_review 12 + security_audit 8 + test 4 + ui 4 + hermes_evidence 1; micro-titles 12/22/20/20/10; first unit RP22.P09.M06.S09, last RP22.P09.M10.S10. All descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data / runtime-closed boundaries verified across registry.js, service.js (freezeCp693Result attestation; every *_runtime_opened and payload flag false), validators.js (descriptor validator asserts claude_is_final_approval=false and all runtime flags false), and model.test.js; both contracts and the validator script project CP00-693 with next_subphase RP23.P00.M00.S01 and historical ids through CP00-692; next-pack-queue live_latest=CP00-693. Claude bounded read-only/non-final (read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes bounded to control-plane evidence packet/receipt only (hermes_runtime_opened=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false; not embedded in product runtime). Review-hardening preconditions met: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid JSON), no forbidden tools, prompt 2555B within 25600B limit, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped evidence. One non-blocking P3 (live pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot fallback, same accepted topology as CP00-690/691/692). No P0/P1/P2. blocks_goal_closeout=false: pack is descriptor-only and does not claim runtime or enterprise trust. Provenance caveat: the 22 validation commands in command-evidence.json are recorded exit 0 and are consistent with the inspected source, but were not independently re-executed under the read-only constraint.

findings.title: Live pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot rather than an independent plan-array entry
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json carries CP00-693 only in live_state (line 36); its packs[] array begins at CP00-694 (line 401). As a result both the contract-validation script (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:385-387) and the model test (packages/integrations-core/test/model.test.js:375-377) resolve the CP00-693 plan pack through the `?? cp693Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so the live pack's coverage is checked against a snapshot embedded in the pack's own manifest rather than an independent plan-array entry. Risk is low: validateExternalIntegrationsICp693Coverage (validators.js:1107-1124) checks that snapshot against the frozen EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP693_REQUIREMENTS constants hard-coded in registry.js:1849-1991 (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, independently re-verified to sum to 84), which serve as the code-reviewed anchor, and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65...; manifest line 2608 == next-pack-queue line 7) indicating common generator provenance. This is the same topology recorded as a non-blocking P3 in the CP00-690, CP00-691, and CP00-692 reviews.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Optionally, a future generator run could emit the current/live pack into plan.packs[] (or a dedicated closed-packs array) so the live pack's coverage is cross-validated against a plan-level entry independent of its own manifest.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as expected, low-risk design; non-blocking. The frozen REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js are the independent coverage anchor and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's ledger SHA, so the verification chain remains sound.
